truck go vroom

8 hours in and I’m a professional speed-limit-ignorer. My truck has more dents than a used soda can, and I still think red lights are just suggestions. I’m basically a high-speed tourist with a cargo full of potatoes. 10/10, would accidentally take a wrong turn to Switzerland again.

Bought ETS2 thinking it was a relaxing trucking game.

Five minutes later I was doing 150 km/h with a fully loaded trailer, blasting music, overtaking everything on the highway like I was in Fast & Furious: Balkan Freight Edition.

Then I slightly touched the guardrail.

My truck launched into the air, flipped 7 times, landed perfectly on the roof, destroyed half the cargo, blocked traffic in 3 countries, and instantly failed the delivery.

Repair bill: €150,000
Bank account: negative numbers I didn’t even know existed
Driver rating: probably legally banned from Europe

Now I’m in debt to the bank, my truck looks like it survived a war, and my GPS lady sounds disappointed in me.

10/10 would ruin a transport company again. Highly recommend.

I will give it a thumbs up only because it is truck sim, but after playing American Truck Sim I am really put off by the constant stopping at the toll booths in Euro Truck Sim. The maps are truncated causing all these toll booths to be close together and because of this you never get to set the cruise control and enjoy the ride, so therefore the game loses it's ability to be casual. in my opinion you could remove about 80% of the toll booths and this game would be a 5 star game as it is now it is a three star game. American Truck sim is 10 times better then the Euro Truck Sim. American Truck Sim is wild and open and free roaming and Euro Truck Sim is restrictive and ridged in its design.

The only RPG that deserves a perfect 10/10. This game is a masterpiece. There are so many things that you can do in this game, I've barely even scratched the surface with 300+ hours in the game. This is the only game where I'd recommend it to all of my friends, regardless if they like RPGs or turn based games. The music, the story, the gameplay, the feeling, the characters, the setting, the dept, the artwork, the animation, the lore, the everything. Every single thing in this game that you do has an impact on where you will end. Every person you spare, every person you kill, it all changes how the game plays out. Buy the game, you won't regret it. If you don't play the game, you are missing out on an opportunity of a lifetime.

If you are curious about, or like anything RPG or D&D related, then do yourself a favor and buy this game. At this time, I have 329 hours on record and am only not near the end. I have done everything that I can in one campaign, but even if you finish it, there so many decisions to go back and change. There is more story in this game than the last 10 games I have played combined. There is ultra satisfying turn based content and character building. I can't say enough about this game. They are still releasing free content, such as new subclasses and quality of life improvements, and Larian is just an amazing studio. If you don't love this game then nothing is wrong with the game, something is wrong with you.

The writing wants to be snarky but falls flat. Because of that most of the characters are punchable.

The gameplay has lots of inconsistencies and feels janky (why do some actions trigger a fight but some other do not ? Why some action need a confirmation click and other do not ?).

The story is forced on you and there are very few choices that actually matter. You will have to interact with this guy, this guy, this guy. And you have to be friend with this guy, this guy and this guy. It takes a lot of effort to have a play through that differs from the intended route.

This game is nothing short of a masterpiece of game design. I was a playtester of the game, feedback and suggestions relating to Forgotten Realms/Dungeons & Dragons lore I made were listened to, the game is so well designed and considers so many interactions even in dialogue that it's simply mind boggling. I have beaten the game 3 times and played dozens of runs that got varying lengths, and every time I always discover something new. The modding support the game has has made so many things possible. This game is a labour of love, and I wouldn't dare say anything but that this is one of my favourite games of all time, and Larian Studios and their adoration for their craft needs to never be forgotten. A game like this comes only once in a blue moon. Fun narrative, amazing replayability, entertaining tactical combat (OR casual if that's your style,) and a deep dive into Dungeons and Dragons that could work even for those unfamiliar with the hobby or the world accociated.

Wow… what a journey. This is easily going into my top 10 games of all time.

First of all, the strongest part of this game is the story. Honestly, it is one of the best stories I have ever experienced in a video game. It’s the kind of story that leaves an emptiness in your heart after finishing it, because the characters and the narrative are incredibly well written and emotional.

Every character you meet feels unique, memorable, and admirable. Each of them has a very distinct personality, almost like characters from a great novel. The dialogue and interactions make you feel truly connected to the world and the people in it.

As for the gameplay, of course it is a CRPG, but unlike many other CRPGs where most of the story is delivered through simple text boxes and static portraits, this game features immersive cutscenes that make the experience feel much more alive and cinematic.

The graphics are good, though not always amazing. For example, even when using an NVMe SSD, I still noticed some texture pop-in, which can be a bit distracting at times.

Overall, this is a 10/10 experience for me. If there is an afterlife, I would probably ask to play this game again.

Rocket League is one of those games that's impossible to put down — I've sunk hundreds of hours into it and still find myself coming back, chasing that perfect aerial or that one clean redirect that makes everything click. The gameplay itself is genuinely brilliant: simple enough to pick up in minutes, but the ceiling on skill is basically infinite, and every goal you score feels earned through pure mechanics rather than luck. That said, it's hard to ignore what Epic did to this game — removing trading, gutting the progression system, and turning what used to be a thriving community into a cash-grab storefront was a gut punch to longtime fans. The toxicity in lobbies is real too, though the quick chat 'Nice shot!' after an own goal somehow never gets old. Despite all that, the core game is still one of the best ever made, and I'd recommend it to anyone who wants a competitive experience unlike anything else — just temper your expectations around everything outside the actual matches.

One of the greatest skill-based ranked games ever made. I've been playing Rocket League for over a decade now and it still feels like the benchmark for pure competitive gameplay. The developers have done a phenomenal job over time with quality-of-life updates and cosmetic additions that keep the game feeling fresh without changing what makes it so good in the first place.

The biggest thing that turns new players away is the skill gap. It looks simple at first glance, but once you get into ranked you realize just how in-depth the gameplay really is. Mechanics, car control, aerials, rotations, game sense, all of it. It takes real time and repetition to build muscle memory, and a lot of people don’t stick around long enough to push through that early learning curve. But if you do, it’s easily one of the most rewarding games out there.

The main downsides are smurfs and the removal of trading, which definitely took away some of that community feel and long-term engagement. Outside of that though, it’s still an incredibly skill based competitive experience, filled with a beautifully toxic community that somehow makes ranked both frustrating and entertaining at the same time.

Rating: 10/10 — one of, if not the greatest skill-based ranked game ever made, and it’s aged insanely well.

I don't like farming simulators, but this is truly exceptional. I'm never bored even while farming or fishing, and delving into caverns legitimately feels dangerous, which I was not expecting from a seemingly innocent game. On top of that, there's some pretty heavy lore implications, though the character developments is where the heart of this game lies. Accumulating wealth in this game is very satisfying, though can sometimes become a drag when you're waiting for a specific event or just gathering resources for a quest. Even so, the reward is almost always worth it, if not for the journey alone.

still a buggy unfinished mess. came across a lot of old problems that didnt change. clipping through the floor, inconsistent problems with the skill tree, and a lot of dialogue choices needing a certain skill level depending on what the scene asks of you. not once has it felt consistent. the level system remains broken as hell.

a player should be able to approach those scenes with those dialogue options naturally, yet it takes days of grinding repetitive side quests that have almost no originality whatsoever. it makes the player easily bored of the game. and even with that grind, you constantly need to halt the story for those lame side quests and spend hours on them.

if this is what it takes to have all dialogue choices at your disposal, then it is not worth supporting this game. franchises like this should take all the time they need before releasing something. at this point cyberpunk just feels like a sandbox game in my opinion.

also still seeing a lot of graphical issues. the game runs smoothly and doesnt crash at all, but if you choose high settings on a decent gaming pc, it should not be delivering potato textures on characters while texture details are set to high.

I've never cried so much to every ending in a game before, never felt so happy to be immersed inside of a games world, never adored the writing and characters + relationships they all have with each other, never had so much fun in 3d combat, never got so much enjoyment and meaning and escape from a game before than Cyberpunk 2077. Truly this game went from being barely playable on all platforms outside of mediocre on PC in December 2020 to being one of the greatest video games of all time. But even switching from a 3060 to a 5070 to a 7900XTX to a 7800XT to now a 4070ti Super my experience never felt terrible, granted its always felt better since after my 3060. I'll probably never find a game that connects to my struggles in life again so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ much and that honestly makes me sad, outside of the fact Ill probably play this game forever. Thank you CD Projekt RED, please don't ruin the next game, I'll never be able to forget the work and dedication your team put into adapting the world into this game, beautifully executing it, messing up, and then not only fixing everything in stride but releasing one of the best DLCs for a game ive ever played. Seeya in the major leagues 😞.

Cyberpunk 2077 is more than just a video game; it is a deep dive into a fascinating and complex world. Playing this game feels like stepping into a living, breathing city where every street has a history. Night City is built with incredible detail, and the game does an amazing job of showing how advanced technology changes everyday life.

One of the best parts of the game is the rich lore and the detailed in-game technology. The game explores how the physical world connects with advanced digital systems, which makes the setting feel very grounded and realistic. Learning about how the cyberware works and mapping out the history of the different corporations is incredibly engaging. The game puts a lot of effort into explaining the technical side of its universe, making it a joy to explore for anyone who appreciates well-thought-out digital and physical networks.

This is one of the best games I’ve ever played. It’s extremely immersive, raw, and gritty. A lot of replayability with different combat builds and dialogue choices. Hell, even the gender of your character changes the story. 

After finishing this game, I felt empty. Not because of the depressive nature of Night City and its outcome, but because there aren’t any other games like this one on the market. So much care, thought, and time went into every aspect of this game. It’s an absolute masterpiece. 

Unpopular opinion, but The Tower ending from the Phantom Liberty DLC is the best and most satisfying ending to this game.
Holy masterpiece. My favorite game so far.

The only real thing I dislike about this game is how odd the ai feels and how some parts feel unpolished/forgotten. But other than that this game is amazing. The world, characters, story, and customization are phenomenal and offer a much more unique experience compared to other open world crime shooters. Try it!

After having finally played it after 6 years since release, I'd give it an 8.0/10 with Phantom Liberty 9.5/10. Cyberpunk 2077 offers amazing world building and an almost sandbox way to play the game. The base game story is compelling but choices don't seem to have as big an impact as I'd like them to have. Phantom Liberty is better than the base game in every aspect and should've been the standard from the start. The biggest downside of this game is the performance; DLSS and FG is basically mandatory to get a decent framerate while keeping the graphics looking good.
mindblowing

I did really enjoy this game, and it is worth the investment (on sale) if you are an RPG lover like myself. HOWEVER, as CD Projekt Red unfortunately also made my favorite game of all time (The Witcher 3) I will say a few things fell short that I know these devs were capable of executing better. Firstly I think perks railroad your gameplay instead of complimenting it. In the Witcher 3 I could utilize a strong sign, some potion perks, weapon perks etc. in tandem with each other. Cyberpunk it becomes more like.....another percentage increase in headshot damage, a little more stealth damage and I have to invest a decent amount of progression to get truly impactful perks. While it isn't bad per se, I think it makes it hard to have a more diverse experience and disincentivizes swapping between playstyles on the fly in favor of min-maxing more specific things. Romance is also lackluster, in a world this large and vibrant and active I shouldn't have only one romance option per orientation. If you are like me and you 100% stuff then late game gets pretty stale as you max your street cred and level pretty well before the end of the game even with the 10 extra levels from DLC which is always unfortunate. Side content (gigs, scanners, jobs) is decent and they did a good job of making even NCPD scanner missions feel important by connecting them through text logs in each district. And yeah, Phantom Liberty was nice with the way it perfectly interweaves into the narrative, not truly feeling like an "add-on." Its a lovely sci-fi sandbox and it is worth playing through in any capacity, but man it could've been even better.

Cyberpunk 2077 is one of the most immersive open-world RPGs I've played. Set in the futuristic and visually stunning Cyberpunk 2077 universe, the game offers a rich story filled with memorable characters, meaningful choices, and excellent voice acting. The city feels alive, with detailed environments, diverse districts, and plenty of activities to discover.

One of the game's strongest aspects is its storytelling. Characters such as Johnny Silverhand leave a lasting impression, and the main storyline is engaging from start to finish. The gameplay allows for multiple playstyles, whether you prefer stealth, hacking, gunplay, or melee combat.

This is one of the best games I have played in a long time. What I liked most about Cyberpunk is the combat and movement mechanics, they are just so smooth and satisfying. There's so much you can do with your build, multiple ways to execute a mission without feeling constrained. Phantom liberty is a no brainer purchase with a much deeper story and bonker weapons. You just have to play this.

Night City sets a new bar when it comes to level of detail and immersion in an open world game. Every inch of the landscape feels like it's been hand crafted. Quite impressive to say the least.

The gameplay is fun. There is a lot of different styles you can take with all the ability/combat trees.

The main story and voice acting is stellar as well. Side quests are hit or miss but everything is voice acted.

Tons of collectables and optional stuff to do.

Cyberpunk really shines if you invest some time into mods as well.

Despite all the positives its got going for it, Cyberpunk still does have some clunky issues and bugs. The game crashed occasionally but not so often that it hindered my gameplay too much. That being said I think the positives far outweigh the cons at this point. I heard it had a really rocky launch which is why I waited so long to try it. 

It's worth it to jump in now for sure.

Decent game. Not an RPG, mostly a visual novel with RPG-lite elements and no impactful dialogue options until the end of the game. The world is empty. Nothing to interact with besides merchants. Gameplay is decent; I enjoyed my sandevistan katana reflex/cool/tech build. But the gameplay loop gets repetitive. you spend 10 minutes killing people and then listen to NPCs drone on for 30 more minutes. The loot system is whatever, enemies all drop the same ♥♥♥♥.  Youll find one legendary weapon and use it for most of the game. I only looted stuff just for cyberware. The AI is dumb, hard mode only buff their damage. Alot of the side objectives are all the same copy-paste open world objectives (escort, kill, steal, listen to NPCs) but some of them actually had a decent story. I did enjoy a lot of the characters though and also leveling up the skill tree.

Not a bad game but it’s not what I was looking for. I really wanted a true Cyberpunk RPG experience.

game

This game is honestly one of the best examples of how a rough start doesn’t define the final stage.

At the beginning, things were honestly not great but the way the developers turned it around deserves real respect. Right now, it’s easily one of the top games.

The main story is very engaging, especially the opening with Jackie who is such a well-written and memorable character that really pulls you into the world. The side missions are just as good (sometimes even better) and almost all of them feel meaningful rather than like filler content.

Gameplay is where the game really shines. You get a lot of freedom and most missions can be approached in multiple ways, depending on your playstyle. It makes the whole experience feel dynamic and very rewarding.

And the DLC? Absolutely A M A Z I N G. One of the best expansions I’ve ever played, high quality, great story and it adds even more depth to the game.

This is a game that deserves a second chance from anyone who gave up on it early. It has grown into something truly special and I genuinely hope even more people give it a try!

Highly recommended 👍

That's an amazing and absolutely unforgettable game.

Cyberpunk 2077 is all about the process. You can spend a few hours in Night City and not even notice how much time has passed. You WANT to explore. Maybe it's just me, but I genuinely wanted to look into every district, discover every possible thing, and just live in that city for a while. Night City feels believable — and at some point, you stop feeling like a player and start feeling like a person living there.

This game has an insane amount of content. There’s always something to do. Gigs, side jobs, random encounters, characters, hidden stories — at times it feels endless. And honestly, trying to finish everything in one playthrough is almost impossible.

But then something interesting happens. When you finally start running out of things to do, the city begins to feel strangely empty. Almost confusing. Like, “come on… this is Night City, there should always be something happening.” And somehow, that feeling fits the game perfectly.

Of course, the game isn’t perfect. The biggest issue for me was the bugs. There are still plenty of them — sometimes small, sometimes really noticeable. Occasionally they can completely break the immersion and make you laugh at a serious moment. Maybe I’m just too used to Rockstar-level polish, but I definitely felt that Cyberpunk could’ve been more refined in terms of quality of the open-world details and overall gameplay interactions, not just "a beautiful picture".

And of course — the ending. The characters are written incredibly well, so whichever path you take, it hits hard. For me, this was a story about change. Everyone has their own V, their own journey, and their own version of Night City.

I definitely recommend this game to anyone who hasn't played it yet.

And for everyone who already finished their journey in Night City — a tight embrace 🫂

P.S. — whoever was responsible for the soundtrack deserves their hands kissed. Some tracks hit harder than entire missions.
